title: Hooks CRUD — untagged hand-authored hook write/update/delete (distinct from owner-scoped) + HookEditor; fitting-owned read-only-with-provenance

tests.summary: 7 passed — create UNTAGGED group (no _garrison) seen as loose by the state model; edit command+matcher; delete + prune empty event; REFUSE edit/delete of a _garrison-owned group (code 'owned', left intact); hand-authored coexists beside an owned group on the same event (owner preserved, mine untagged); dispatch round-trip; event+command validation

typecheck.summary: 0 errors (hooks-crud + quarters-detail hook branch + quarters dispatch hook actions + HookEditor + registry editable/deleteBody)

build.summary: covered by the C1 build + re-verified at the global gate

e2e.summary: 4 passed — the new Hooks test: hand-authored SessionStart#0 shows Edit+Remove and prefills its command on edit; fitting-owned Stop#0 shows 'fitting-owned · fitting:session-view' with NO edit/delete; create→edit→delete a fresh Notification hook; seeded groups intact

by: vision screenshots c4-hooks-list.png + c4-hooks-new-form.png vs the shell
designAudit.notes: The writer-of-record invariant is visible in one screen: LOOSE hand-authored row with Edit+Remove vs OWNED fitting row that is read-only with provenance. New-hook form has event (datalist of common events), optional matcher, command, optional timeout. Matches the shell; no emoji

video.note: Covered by the VERIFIED consolidated 'Manage everything from Quarters' walkthrough (11 beats, all vision-verified, run not flagged). This slice's own re-runnable committed e2e is the per-slice correctness gate.

notes: The most delicate slice. hooks-crud.ts adds hand-authored (UNTAGGED) hook write/update/delete — deliberately distinct from claude-settings-file's owner-scoped helpers: a hand-authored hook must stay untagged or the state model would misclassify it as fitting-owned. Both update and delete REFUSE a _garrison-tagged group (read-only here; uninstall the fitting to change it), enforced server-side; the UI also hides Edit (editable predicate) and Delete (deleteBody null) for owned hooks and shows provenance instead. claude-settings-file.ts (the delicate single settings writer) was left untouched — new logic lives in hooks-crud.ts.
